candidate_policy: Skip rows with blocked_by until ready_when is satisfied.
Skip slice_size=umbrella rows until they are split.
Default cmd/autoloop runs cap eligible roadmap work at Phase 3 unless MAX_PHASE is explicitly overridden.
MAX_AGENTS is a safety cap: if fewer metadata-ready rows are available, run fewer workers instead of selecting filler or random work.
Each worker runs in an isolated git worktree under RUN_ROOT/worktrees and promotion rejects committed paths outside the selected row's write_scope.
When git worktrees are available and MAX_AGENTS is greater than 1, cmd/autoloop launches selected workers concurrently, then validates and promotes each branch through the same ledgered safety gates.
After all promotions, cmd/autoloop runs the mandatory post-promotion full-suite gate before emitting run_completed or health_updated.
On post-promotion gate failure, cmd/autoloop starts one backend repair attempt by default, requires the checkout to be clean, reruns the suite, and records final health only if the gate passes.
Prefer contract rows with write_scope, test_commands, and done_signal.
Inject selected progress metadata into the worker prompt instead of asking workers to rescan the whole roadmap.

acceptance: Interrupted turns record a skipped-sync reason and do not create new GONCHO conclusions.
Completed turns still sync/extract normally.
Operator status can distinguish skipped interrupted sync from extractor failures.
items.note: TDD landed: interrupted turns are marked as skipped with reason=interrupted, stay out of extractor and Honcho-compatible turn reads, and create no goncho_conclusions; completed turns still mark ready and extract normally. Fixtures cover internal/memory/interrupted_sync_test.go plus skipped-row guards in the memory session catalog and GONCHO service. Verified with go test ./internal/kernel ./internal/memory ./internal/goncho -count=1.
done_signal: Interrupted-turn fixtures prove partial memory observations are skipped while completed turns still sync.

items.name: Honcho-compatible scope/source tool schema

Deny-path fixtures and operator evidence remain separate 3.E.7 slices.

items.name: parent_session_id lineage for compression splits

acceptance: session.Metadata carries parent_session_id and lineage_kind while root sessions remain parentless by default.

SessionIndexMirror or an equivalent read model exposes parent/child/orphan lineage for operator audit.
items.note: Complete: internal/session.Metadata now persists parent_session_id plus lineage_kind, roots default to primary without a parent, BoltMap and MemMap reject self-parent and parent-chain loops, ResolveLineageTip walks append-only compression descendants without rewriting ancestor metadata, and SessionIndexMirror renders child/orphan lineage status for operator audit. Compression execution, gateway resume routing, and lineage-aware search remain separate dependent rows.
done_signal: Session lineage fixtures prove append-only parent metadata, loop rejection, and operator-visible orphan status without implementing compression.

items.name: Gateway resume follows compression continuation

acceptance: Resume by title or root ID follows a compression child chain to the newest live descendant.
Already-current session checks compare against the resolved descendant, not the stale root.
Transcript loading and session switching use the resolved descendant while operator output still names the requested session.
items.note: Complete: TDD added internal/gateway/resume_continuation_test.go proving stored resume roots follow compression lineage to the newest live descendant before gateway submit, fork children are ignored, unresolved continuation chains fall back to the requested root with visible resume status, and the session context still names the requested stale root while using the resolved descendant as the live SessionID.

items.name: Goncho context representation options

items.name: Goncho search filter grammar

items.name: Goncho queue status read model
items.contract: Gormes exposes Honcho-style representation, summary, and dream work-unit queue status as observability, not synchronization

Docs and CLI output state that queue status is for observability and debugging, not waiting for completion.
items.note: TDD landed: Goncho exposes a Honcho-style zero-state queue status read model for representation, summary, and dream work units with completed_work_units, in_progress_work_units, pending_work_units, total_work_units, and optional per-session details. Memory status and Goncho doctor output include extractor queue status alongside Goncho work-unit counts and explicitly frame queue status as observability/debugging evidence, not a synchronization contract or queue-drain wait condition.

items.name: Goncho summary context budget
items.contract: Session summaries use Honcho's short/long cadence and 40/60 context budget without double-billing last-N-turn recall
items.degraded_mode: When summaries are unavailable or too large for the token budget, context returns recent messages plus explicit summary_absent evidence.

not_ready_when: The slice rewrites RecallProvider.GetContext or merges summaries into the existing memory-context fence instead of adding a separate Goncho context component.
acceptance: Schema stores one short and one long summary slot per session with last-covered message and token count.
Short summaries trigger every 20 messages and long summaries every 60 messages by default.
Service.Context allocates 40 percent of the token budget to summary and 60 percent to recent messages when summary=true.
summary=false fills the budget with recent messages only.
items.note: TDD landed: Goncho now stores one short and one long session summary slot in goncho_session_summaries, refreshes deterministic short/long slots at the Honcho default 20/60 message cadence, exposes summary/tokens/search_query context options, allocates summary=true context with a 40 percent summary budget and 60 percent recent-message budget without reusing messages covered by the selected summary, lets summary=false spend the token budget on recent messages only, and reports summary_absent evidence when no requested summary can fit.
done_signal: Summary context fixtures prove short/long cadence, token-budget allocation, summary=false behavior, and visible degradation when no summary can fit.

items.name: Goncho dialectic chat contract

The non-streaming response shape is content-only and matches Honcho's OpenAPI response contract.
stream=true and target-specific reasoning gaps return explicit degraded evidence until implemented.
honcho_reasoning remains available for backward compatibility.
items.note: Honcho docs expose peer.chat as the slow query-specific reasoning path; newer host integrations call this honcho_chat or chat. Gormes should add the alias and contract before porting the full dialectic tool loop.
done_signal: Chat contract tests prove request validation, default reasoning level, response shape, host-compatible tool alias, and explicit streaming degradation.

items.name: Goncho file upload import ingestion
items.contract: Goncho can import text-like memory files as session messages without persisting original file bytes, matching Honcho's file-upload migration model

Original file bytes are not persisted.
created_at, metadata, and configuration are preserved when provided.
Runtime chunk size follows Honcho source settings.MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE at 25000 characters unless upstream changes that setting.

done_signal: File import fixtures prove supported formats become ordinary messages, unsupported formats fail before writes, and original files are not persisted.

items.name: Goncho operator diagnostics contract
items.contract: Gormes exposes a Honcho-inspired Goncho doctor path that checks memory topology, queues, config, and degraded modes without requiring operators to inspect raw tables
items.degraded_mode: Missing optional model/provider features are reported as degraded capability rows, not startup failures, unless a requested command needs them.
ready_when: Topology rules define the expected workspace, peer, session, and observation defaults.
not_ready_when: The slice reaches out to upstream Honcho, external network services, or hosted LLMs by default.
acceptance: A human-readable command reports config, schema, session catalog, tool registration, context dry-run, queue status, conclusion availability, summary availability, provider readiness, and degraded modes.
A JSON output mode is available for autoloop and planner consumption.
Exit code 0 means healthy or explicitly degraded, 1 means client/config issue, 2 means runtime/storage issue, and 3 means auth/provider issue.
Zero-state databases report no data without failing.
The command reuses existing memory status where possible instead of adding a second status model.
items.note: TDD landed: `gormes goncho doctor` reports config paths, schema/table presence, session catalog state, Honcho-compatible tool registration, context dry-run output, extractor and Goncho queue status, conclusion and summary availability, provider readiness, and visible degraded modes without external Honcho, network services, or hosted LLM calls by default. JSON output is available for autoloop/planner consumption; exit-code fixtures cover 0 healthy/degraded, 1 client/config, 2 runtime/storage, and 3 auth/provider. `gormes memory status` now includes the observability-only Goncho representation/summary/dream zero-state alongside the existing extractor queue.
done_signal: Doctor fixtures prove text and JSON output, exit-code mapping, zero-state behavior, and visible degraded capability reporting.

items.name: Goncho streaming chat persistence contract
items.contract: Goncho streaming chat stores the final assistant response once and never turns partial stream chunks into memory
items.degraded_mode: Until streaming transport exists, stream=true returns explicit unsupported evidence while non-streaming chat keeps the Honcho-compatible response contract.
ready_when: honcho_chat or equivalent dialectic chat params and response shape are fixture-locked.
not_ready_when: The slice stores stream chunks as messages, creates synthetic assistant turns before completion, or changes honcho_context behavior.
unblocks: Hugo docs examples for streaming memory behavior
acceptance: Streaming response handlers accumulate chunks into a final response before persistence.
Partial chunks are never written as memory messages.
Interrupted streams record interruption evidence and do not flush incomplete assistant content.
Successful streamed assistant responses are stored exactly once with the same session and assistant peer as non-streaming chat.
Token/counting metadata can be attached after completion without affecting the stored text.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed internal/goncho/streaming_chat_persistence_test.go. The fixture proves stream=true degraded chat persists the final assistant response once, streaming handlers buffer chunks until completion, token metadata attaches after completion without mutating stored text, and interrupted streams return evidence without flushing partial assistant content to memory.
done_signal: Streaming fixtures prove completed responses are persisted once and interrupted or partial chunks cannot pollute memory.

items.name: Goncho configuration namespace
items.contract: Gormes owns a Go-native [goncho] configuration namespace that maps Honcho runtime limits and feature gates into existing config loading

Environment variables use the GORMES_GONCHO_ prefix.

Defaults mirror Honcho docs where applicable: max_message_size=25000, max_file_size=5242880, get_context_max_tokens=100000, deriver_workers=1, representation_batch_max_tokens=1024, and dream_enabled=false until fixtures exist.
Invalid reasoning levels and negative limits fail config validation.
Doctor output includes the effective Goncho config with secrets redacted.

done_signal: Config fixtures prove defaults, GORMES_GONCHO_* overrides, validation failures, and doctor visibility for effective Goncho settings.

4.name: Phase 4 — The Brain Transplant
4.deliverable: Native Go agent orchestrator + prompt builder
4.dependency_note: Build priority: 2.E0 deterministic subagent runtime -> 2.G static skills + reviewed candidate flow -> runner-enforced delegation policy + wider gateway surface -> native agent loop

Detection covers DeepSeek provider name, model substring, and `api.deepseek.com` host plus Kimi/Moonshot provider/host signals. Explicit `reasoning_content` and normalized `reasoning` echoes are preserved on thinking-provider tool-call turns, non-tool assistant turns and generic providers are left unpadded, ordinary assistant content is unchanged, and provider status exposes the reasoning_content padding/repair degraded mode.

items.name: Anthropic

items.name: Bedrock
items.note: Umbrella only: not landed on main. Five codexu/* worker attempts on 2026-04-23 produced candidate Bedrock adapters (`feat(phase4): add bedrock provider adapter` x2, `feat(provider): add bedrock client`, `feat(provider): add native Bedrock adapter`, `feat(gormes): add native bedrock provider adapter`) but none merged — no `bedrock_*.go` file is tracked in `internal/hermes/` and `grep -i bedrock|converse[sS]tream` over that package returns zero matches. Execute via the three dependency-ordered TDD slices below (payload mapping -> stream decoding -> SigV4/credential seam) before another full-rewrite attempt.

items.name: Bedrock Converse payload mapping (no AWS SDK)
items.contract: Pure Bedrock Converse request mapping over the shared provider message/tool contract
items.degraded_mode: Provider status reports Bedrock as unavailable until request mapping fixtures pass and credential wiring lands.

Fixture-only unit tests must prove (a) base URL and model-family selection over the OpenAI-compatible client seam, (b) attribution-header emission, (c) retryable vs fatal error mapping through `internal/hermes/errors.go`, and (d) usage/pricing accounting against captured response fixtures — no live `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in tests. TDD scope: upstream Codex is an OpenAI-Codex OAuth surface over the Responses API (not standard chat.completions), so the adapter must wrap `/v1/responses` at `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex` — upstream surface lives at `agent/auxiliary_client.py:483 CodexAuxiliaryClient` / `:313 _CodexCompletionsAdapter` / `:501 _AsyncCodexCompletionsAdapter` / `:1038 _try_codex` (Responses→chat-completions shim, empty-output backfill from stream events, `gpt-5.2-codex` vs `gpt-5.3-codex` fallback), and the auth seam lives at `hermes_cli/auth.py:1451 refresh_codex_oauth_pure` / `:1546 _refresh_codex_auth_tokens` / `:1567 _import_codex_cli_tokens` / `:1601 resolve_codex_runtime_credentials` / `:3023 _codex_device_code_login`, with model enumeration at `hermes_cli/codex_models.py:147 get_codex_model_ids`. On main today: `internal/kernel/frame.go:84` advertises a `PlatformEvent.Model` field with a doc-comment that references a `Config.ModelSelector` that does not exist; `internal/kernel/kernel.go:179` calls `runTurn(ctx, e.Text, e.SessionContext, e.CronJobID)` and silently drops `e.Model`; `internal/kernel/kernel.go:296` builds the `hermes.ChatRequest` with `Model: k.cfg.Model` (no per-turn override); `internal/kernel/model_selection.go` does not exist (`git cat-file -e HEAD:internal/kernel/model_selection.go` returns 128); and `grep -rn 'activeModel\|ModelSelector\|selectModel' internal/kernel` returns zero hits. items.name: Deterministic helper-file ports (banner/output/tips/webhook/dump)
items.note: TDD-first prerequisite slice for the larger 5.O command-group slices below. Port the pure-function helper surface from upstream hermes_cli as foundational primitives before any command-group or active-turn policy work lands on top: `hermes_cli/banner.py` -> `internal/cli/banner.go` (FormatContextLength, DisplayToolsetName, FormatBannerVersionLabel), `hermes_cli/cli_output.py` -> `internal/cli/output.go` (shared print/prompt helpers), `hermes_cli/tips.py` -> `internal/cli/tips.go` (tip corpus + GetRandomTip/PickRandomTip selector), `hermes_cli/webhook.py` -> `internal/cli/webhook.go` (deterministic helpers), `hermes_cli/dump.py` -> `internal/cli/dump.go` (deterministic helpers). Each helper file lands behind pure-function unit tests with fixed inputs/outputs — no terminal/TTY coupling, no config file reads, no time-of-day non-determinism. Evidence of granularity mismatch on main: as of planner run 2026-04-24, `git log --all --since='2026-04-23' --oneline | grep -cE 'feat\(cli\)|feat\(tuigateway\)'` reports **54 worker commits** across codexu/* branches attempting these helper ports with **zero** merged to main, and `internal/cli/` currently contains only `parity.go`/`root_test.go` — the larger command-group slices (`CLI command registry parity + active-turn busy policy`, `Config/profile/auth/setup`, `Gateway/platform/webhook/cron`, `Diagnostics/backup/logs/status`) are too big a jump from the current tree and must not consume the helper-file ports. Keep this slice strictly additive and pure-function — no command-registry wiring in this slice.

items.name: PTY bridge protocol adapter
items.contract: Dashboard/TUI PTY sessions expose bounded read, write, resize, close, and unavailable-state behavior through a testable adapter
items.degraded_mode: Dashboard or CLI status reports PTY unavailable instead of falling back to unsafe shell execution.
ready_when: Deterministic CLI helper ports are understood and PTY behavior can be isolated from the web dashboard transport.
not_ready_when: The slice starts the web dashboard, opens network listeners, or binds to a real TUI process in unit tests.
unblocks: SSE streaming to Bubble Tea TUI
acceptance: Reads are bounded by timeout and chunk size.
Writes and resize messages validate input before reaching the PTY.
Unsupported platforms return PtyUnavailable-style errors without starting a shell.
items.note: Complete: TDD landed internal/cli/pty_bridge_test.go plus a small internal/cli PTY adapter. Fixtures prove bounded read timeout/chunk behavior, write forwarding with pre-session validation, resize escape validation and winsize forwarding, idempotent close/child termination, and ErrPtyUnavailable degradation before spawn on unsupported platforms without dashboard, network, or live TUI dependencies.
done_signal: PTY bridge fixtures prove read/write/resize/close/unavailable behavior without network or live dashboard dependencies.

items.name: Busy command guard for compression and long CLI actions

not_ready_when: Busy state is implemented only for /compress or only in the visual TUI without a command-layer invariant.

acceptance: /compress and other long-running command handlers set and clear busy state even on error.
User input during busy command execution returns a visible busy response.
The guard does not block gateway-safe commands that explicitly declare active-turn bypass.
items.note: Hermes now wraps `/compress` in a busy command guard. Track the behavior as a CLI policy slice so Gormes does not accept overlapping input while compression or other long-running commands mutate context.
done_signal: Busy-command fixtures prove overlapping input is rejected while long-running CLI commands execute and busy state clears on every exit path.

This slice freezes the public install-surface contract so downstream docs/site builds do not regress.

items.name: Deterministic helper-file ports (tool-progress/image/completion-path/personality/platform-event)
items.note: TDD-first prerequisite slice for the `SSE streaming to Bubble Tea TUI` and API-server slices below. Port the pure-function helper surface from upstream `tui_gateway/` as foundational primitives before any transport/lifecycle work lands: `_load_tool_progress_mode` -> ParseToolProgressMode/ToolProgressEnabled, `_normalize_completion_path` -> NormalizeCompletionPath, `_estimate_image_tokens`/`_image_meta` -> EstimateImageTokens/ImageMeta/ReadImageMeta, upstream tool-progress helpers -> FormatToolDuration/CountList/ToolSummary, personality validator + renderer, and the upstream `PlatformEvent` struct surface for the remote Bubble Tea TUI. Each helper lands behind pure-function unit tests with fixed inputs/outputs — no HTTP, no SSE, no JSON-RPC dispatch, no Bubble Tea program in this slice. Evidence of granularity mismatch on main: as of planner run 2026-04-24, `internal/tuigateway/` does not exist in the tree, and `git log --all --since='2026-04-23'` shows **repeated worker attempts** on codexu/* branches for each of these helpers (tool-progress mode, completion-path, image primitives, tool-progress helpers, personality validator, PlatformEvent, RemoteClient, JSONRPCDispatcher, NewGatewayMux) with **zero** merged to main — confirming the `SSE streaming to Bubble Tea TUI` slice is too big a jump from the current tree and must not consume the helper-file ports. Keep this slice strictly additive and pure-function — no NewRemoteClient, no NewGatewayMux, no SSE dial in this slice; those land in the SSE-streaming slice once the primitives are green.

items.name: API server disconnect snapshot persistence
items.contract: Streaming disconnects and server cancellations persist incomplete Responses snapshots when store=true

not_ready_when: Client disconnects lose response IDs or previous_response_id chains.

acceptance: Connection reset during stream interrupts the agent and stores an incomplete response snapshot when store=true.
async cancellation stores the same incomplete snapshot before returning cancellation.

items.note: Upstream Hermes now persists incomplete Responses snapshots on client disconnect and asyncio cancellation. Keep this as a separate resilience slice after the stateful Responses store exists.
done_signal: Disconnect/cancellation fixtures prove store=true responses retain incomplete snapshots and interrupt running turns.

items.name: Strict-mode CWD + interpreter parity
items.note: TDD slice 2 of 4: strict mode keeps today's behavior frozen — isolated tmpdir CWD plus the canonical interpreter resolved via `LocalCodeSandbox.resolveRuntime`. Fixtures must prove the 5.K blocked-result envelope (status, error, filesystem_access, network_access) is unchanged and that relative imports/filesystem access outside the tmpdir continue to fail. This slice exists only to pin the reference behavior before project-mode lands.

items.name: Project-mode CWD + active venv detection
items.note: TDD slice 3 of 4: port upstream `_resolve_python_binary`/`_resolve_cwd` project semantics — session's working directory for CWD and the active virtualenv/conda python when present, falling back to the resolved system interpreter. Unit tests must drive resolution via injected env (`VIRTUAL_ENV`, `CONDA_PREFIX`), not the real host. Do not widen the 5.K filesystem/network blocking contract — project mode still flows through the same `sandboxGuardReason` gate.

items.name: Default mode selection + config cut-over
items.note: TDD slice 4 of 4: pick and document Gormes' default mode (recommend `strict` given the self-hosted security-first posture, with `project` opt-in via config). Wire the resolver into `NewExecuteCodeTool` so callers get the right mode without per-call arguments, add a regression test that the 5.K `Sandboxed exec` blocked-result envelope is byte-identical across modes, and update the rationale in `docs/content/building-gormes/core-systems/tool-execution.md` so operators understand the trade-off before flipping the default.

6.name: Phase 6 — The Learning Loop (Soul)
6.deliverable: Native skill extraction. Compounding intelligence. The feature Hermes doesn't have.

items.name: Heuristic or LLM-scored signal
items.note: TDD: start with deterministic local signals from transcript length, tool-call count, retries, edits, and operator feedback before adding any LLM scorer, so learning-loop triggers are explainable.

items.name: LLM-assisted pattern distillation
items.note: TDD: define the extraction input bundle and output schema with fake model fixtures, including rejection of secrets, one-off tasks, and implementation-specific noise before live generation.

items.name: Portable SKILL.md format
items.contract: Reviewed skill-as-code storage format
items.degraded_mode: Skill status excludes unreviewed or invalid drafts from prompt injection and records resolver or metadata failures.
items.fixture: internal/skills SKILL.md metadata, provenance, and review-state fixtures
ready_when: Phase 2.G skills runtime is complete and the parser/store seam is stable enough for versioned metadata.
not_ready_when: Generated drafts are allowed into prompt injection without explicit review metadata.
acceptance: SKILL.md metadata includes version, provenance, review state, triggers, and exclusions.
Invalid, disabled, or unreviewed drafts never enter prompt injection.
Atomic writes preserve human-editable skill files and version history.
items.note: TDD: extend the Phase 2.G parser/store contract with versioned metadata, provenance, review state, and atomic writes while preserving human-editable SKILL.md files.
done_signal: SKILL.md metadata fixtures prove invalid, disabled, or unreviewed drafts stay out of prompt injection.

items.name: Hybrid lexical + semantic lookup
items.note: TDD: combine lexical tags with Phase 3 semantic embeddings behind a deterministic scorer, proving disabled/unreviewed skills stay excluded from prompt injection.

items.name: Skill effectiveness scoring
items.note: TDD: persist skill-use outcomes, explicit operator feedback, and decay/weight adjustments as auditable records before any automatic promotion or demotion behavior.

items.name: TUI + Telegram browsing
items.note: TDD: expose browse/edit/disable/review flows through TUI and gateway commands after the store and feedback records are stable, with permission and active-turn behavior pinned by tests.

7.name: Phase 7 — Paused Channel Backlog
7.deliverable: Deferred non-priority channel adapters after Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, and WeChat stabilize

items.name: BlueBubbles iMessage bubble formatting parity
items.contract: BlueBubbles outbound iMessage sends are non-editable, markdown-stripped, paragraph-split bubbles without pagination suffixes
items.degraded_mode: BlueBubbles remains a usable first-pass adapter, but long replies may still arrive as one stripped text send until paragraph splitting and suffix-free chunking are fixture-locked.
ready_when: The first-pass BlueBubbles adapter already owns Send, markdown stripping, cached GUID resolution, and home-channel fallback in internal/channels/bluebubbles.
not_ready_when: The slice attempts to add live BlueBubbles HTTP/webhook registration, attachment download, reactions, typing indicators, or edit-message support.
acceptance: Send splits blank-line-separated paragraphs into separate SendText calls while preserving existing chat GUID resolution and home-channel fallback.
Long paragraph chunks omit `(n/m)` pagination suffixes and concatenate back to the stripped original text.
Bot does not implement gateway.MessageEditor or gateway.PlaceholderCapable, preserving non-editable iMessage semantics.
items.note: Upstream Hermes f731c2c2 changed BlueBubbles to `SUPPORTS_MESSAGE_EDITING = False`, split blank-line paragraphs into separate iMessage bubbles, and removed pagination suffixes from chunked sends. Gormes' shipped first-pass adapter has the right package boundary but still sends one stripped body, so this is a narrow parity slice.
done_signal: BlueBubbles adapter tests prove paragraph-to-bubble sends, suffix-free chunking, and no edit/placeholder capability.
